what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment of type board_items that targets a specific board item in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and has no Markdown body.
It declares an attachment id att-fcff993a.
The target is a board://item-d4c2d41a URI.
standouts: References a board item via a board:// URI scheme
Attachment type is board_items in the dragnet-development channel
Contains only YAML frontmatter metadata and no prose body
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Uses a board:// URI to identify the target board item
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset
File name follows the att-<uuid>.md pattern
notes: This is a lightweight metadata stub linking an attachment record to a board item; the actual payload lives elsewhere.
questions: What content or artifact does board://item-d4c2d41a represent?
Is this attachment meant to be a comment, reference, or embedded item on the board?
What system consumes the board:// URI?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated in the file; its structure is consistent with recording a board-item attachment reference.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, queryable record that a specific attachment in the dragnet-development channel is associated with a particular board item.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme to target a board item from a channel attachment is a distinct cross-substrate reference pattern.
contribution: Provides a machine-readable attachment-to-board-item linkage with timestamped provenance, which a fused system can use to correlate channel discussions with board items.
summary_for_embedding: YAML-frontmatter attachment metadata linking att-fcff993a in the dragnet-development channel to board item item-d4c2d41a with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record that a channel attachment refers to a specific board item for later retrieval and correlation.
